kind: structural: extend pass96 Bluestein plan reuse to rfft/irfft (bit-exact)
lever_summary: Apply the pass96 FFTW-style BluesteinPlan (chirp table + kernel FFT, built once) + reusable BluesteinScratch to eval_rfft and eval_irfft for non-power-of-two transform lengths, instead of one-shot fft_1d_into/ifft_1d_into per row.
method: same-bench A/B; baseline = stashed per-row Bluestein; normalized by eval/fft_1000_complex128 (eval_fft path, unaffected by this rfft/irfft change) calibration.

change.lever: eval_rfft/eval_irfft build a BluesteinPlan once for non-pow2 fft_length and call plan.apply_into per row (forward plan for rfft, inverse for irfft). Added eval/rfft_batch_64x1000 bench.

isomorphism.type: BIT-IDENTICAL to committed per-row Bluestein (plan hoisted out of the row loop); 30 fft tests + JAX oracle (incl rfft/irfft) pass

rejected_sub_lever: real-FFT half-size PACKING for rfft (pack N reals into N/2 complex + unpack): measured REGRESSION at N=256 (~1.18x slower normalized), ~1.05x at N=4096 — unpack + buffers offset the half-size FFT saving. Reverted, not shipped.

validation.name: fft_tests
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-lax --release fft

validation.name: jax_oracle
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-conformance --release --test linalg_fft_oracle_parity

validation.name: clippy
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-lax --release --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings

note: 1.31x bit-exact completion of the FFT-family plan reuse (pass96 fft/ifft -> pass97 rfft/irfft). The FFT vein's big algorithmic wins (pass95 12.8x non-pow2, pass96 2.17x batched fft) are realized; remaining FFT levers are sub-2x. Next big swing should be a different primitive.
